2605.20149.title: Less Back-and-Forth: A Comparative Study of Structured Prompting
2605.20149.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are widely used for open-ended tasks, but underspecified prompts can lead to low-quality answers and additional interaction. This paper studies whether structured prompt design improves response quality while reducing user effort. We compare three prompt conditions: a raw prompt, a checklist-improved prompt, and a clarifying-question prompt. We evaluate these conditions across four task types--summarization, planning, explanation, and coding--using three LLM systems: ChatGPT, Claude, and Grok. Each output is scored with a unified rubric covering task completion, correctness, compliance, and clarity. Checklist-improved prompts achieved the highest mean rubric score, 7.50 out of 8, compared with 5.67 for raw prompts and 6.67 for clarifying-question prompts. Checklist prompts also produced the best quality-effort tradeoff, using fewer average tokens than both raw and clarifying prompts. These results suggest that a simple prompt checklist can improve LLM responses while reducing unnecessary interaction.
2605.20149.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）廣泛用於開放式任務，但不明確的提示可能導致低質量的答案和額外的互動。本文研究結構化提示設計是否能改善回應質量，同時減少用戶的努力。我們比較了三種提示條件：原始提示、經檢查表改進的提示和澄清問題提示。我們在四種類型的任務中評估這些條件——摘要、規劃、解釋和編碼——使用三個LLM系統：ChatGPT、Claude和Grok。每個輸出都根據一個統一的評分標準進行評分，涵蓋任務完成度、正確性、合規性和清晰度。經檢查表改進的提示達到了最高的平均評分，為7.50（滿分8分），相比之下，原始提示為5.67，澄清問題提示為6.67。檢查表提示還產生了最佳的質量-努力權衡，平均使用的標記數量少於原始提示和澄清提示。這些結果表明，簡單的提示檢查表可以改善LLM的回應，同時減少不必要的互動。
2605.20149.author: Saurav Ghosh et.al.
2605.20149.authors: Saurav Ghosh, Gabriella Polach, Abdou Sow
